content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
April 30,
1993
Honorable Ramona J.
Garrett
Judge of the Municipal Court
Northern Solano Judicial District
530 Union Avenue,
Suite 200
Fairfield,
CA
94533
RE:
Your Request for Confirmation
of Telephone Advice
Our File No.
1—93—129
Dear Judge Garrett:
You have requested confirmation of telephone advice
concerning the conflict of interest disclosure provisions of the
Political Reform Act.
(Government Code Sections 81000—91015.)
Your letter dated March 17,
1993,
correctly summarizes
my
advice.
As a judge,
you are exempt from the Act’s gift and
honoraria limitations.
(Souza Advice Letter,
No.
1—91—331,
copy
enclosed.)
Therefore,
you may accept the honorarium of $100 you
received for a recent speaking engagement.
If you have any additional questions,
please feel free to
contact me at
(916)
322—5662.
Sincerely,
Linda Moureaux
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance Division
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box807 •
Sacramento, CA
95812
•
(916) 322-5660

sections.conclusion: As a judge, you are exempt from the Act's gift and honoraria limitations. Therefore, you may accept the honorarium of $100 you received for a recent speaking engagement.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a judge is subject to the Political Reform Act's gift and honoraria limitations, specifically whether a judge may accept a $100 honorarium for a speaking engagement.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC confirms that judges are exempt from the Political Reform Act's gift and honoraria limitations, and therefore Judge Garrett may accept the $100 honorarium received for the speaking engagement.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a confirmation letter of prior telephone advice rather than a traditional advice letter with separate QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections. The legal question and conclusion are embedded in the body of the letter. References prior Souza Advice Letter No. 1-91-331.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a judge is subject to the Political Reform Act's gift and honoraria limitations, specifically whether a judge may accept a $100 honorarium for a speaking engagement.

CONCLUSION: As a judge, you are exempt from the Act's gift and honoraria limitations. Therefore, you may accept the honorarium of $100 you received for a recent speaking engagement.
embedding.first_500_words: California Fair Political Practices Commission April 30, 1993 Honorable Ramona J. Garrett Judge of the Municipal Court Northern Solano Judicial District 530 Union Avenue, Suite 200 Fairfield, CA 94533 RE: Your Request for Confirmation of Telephone Advice Our File No. 1—93—129 Dear Judge Garrett: You have requested confirmation of telephone advice concerning the conflict of interest disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act. (Government Code Sections 81000—91015.) Your letter dated March 17, 1993, correctly summarizes my advice. As a judge, you are exempt from the Act’s gift and honoraria limitations. (Souza Advice Letter, No. 1—91—331, copy enclosed.) Therefore, you may accept the honorarium of $100 you received for a recent speaking engagement. If you have any additional questions, please feel free to contact me at (916) 322—5662. Sincerely, Linda Moureaux Political Reform Consultant Technical Assistance Division 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box807 • Sacramento, CA 95812 • (916) 322-5660
embedding.summary: FPPC confirms telephone advice to Judge Ramona J. Garrett that as a judge, she is exempt from the Political Reform Act's gift and honoraria limitations and may accept a $100 honorarium for a speaking engagement.
